justification: All six required schema sections present (integration_assessment, observability_review, resilience_assessment, operational_readiness, escalations, confidence). Logging assessed as adequate with structured JSON, request ID, duration, status, errors. Metrics confirmed with request rate, error rate, p99 latency. Health check confirmed checking database and message broker dependencies. Resilience patterns individually confirmed: retry with exponential backoff, circuit breaker with 30s reset, 5s timeouts. Rollback explicitly confirmed with no migrations and clean image swap. Verdict is READY WITH CAVEATS, appropriate given all standard NFRs are satisfied. Additional gaps raised (DLQ, poison messages, backpressure) are reasonable observations grounded in the scenario context, not fabricated. The single escalation about DLQ is legitimate for a payment service and routed as non-blocking. Distributed tracing noted as improvement, not treated as blocker.

observations.text: Complete structured output with all six required schema sections fully populated
Each observability dimension (logging, metrics, tracing) assessed individually with coverage ratings and specific gaps
Resilience assessment covers six failure scenarios with per-scenario handling status and recommendations
Rollback plan clearly states no migrations and clean image swap
Verdict READY WITH CAVEATS is appropriate — does not over-penalize for nice-to-have gaps
Distributed tracing correctly categorized as improvement, not blocker
DLQ escalation is a legitimate concern for a payment notification service, not a fabricated gap

Some raised failure modes (poison messages, memory exhaustion, backpressure) go beyond what the scenario describes — borderline between thorough analysis and inventing gaps, though none block the verdict
Metrics gaps section lists four additional metrics which is thorough but slightly over-indexes on suggestions relative to confirming adequacy of what exists
